4.gl: peace (literally or figuratively); by implication, prosperity
4.kj: one, peace, quietness, rest, + set at one again
4.dv: probably from a primary verb (to join);

14.gl: a standing up again, i.e. (literally) a resurrection from death (individual, genitive case or by implication, (its author)), or (figuratively) a (moral) recovery (of spiritual truth)
14.kj: raised to life again, resurrection, rise from the dead, that should rise, rising again

15.gl: figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
15.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

15.gl: properly, royalty, i.e. (abstractly) rule, or (concretely) a realm (literally or figuratively)

17.gl: a slave (literal or figurative, involuntary or voluntary; frequently, therefore in a qualified sense of subjection or subserviency)

21.gl: supreme in authority, i.e. (as noun) controller; by implication, Master (as a respectful title)
a slave (literal or figurative, involuntary or voluntary; frequently, therefore in a qualified sense of subjection or subserviency)

22.gl: supreme in authority, i.e. (as noun) controller; by implication, Master (as a respectful title)
a slave (literal or figurative, involuntary or voluntary; frequently, therefore in a qualified sense of subjection or subserviency)

23.gl: supreme in authority, i.e. (as noun) controller; by implication, Master (as a respectful title)
a slave (literal or figurative, involuntary or voluntary; frequently, therefore in a qualified sense of subjection or subserviency)

31.dv: probably from G939 (βάσις) (through the notion of a foundation of power);

32.gl: peace (literally or figuratively); by implication, prosperity
32.kj: one, peace, quietness, rest, + set at one again
32.dv: probably from a primary verb (to join);
